user.title: NASA FIRMS Fires
user.description: Configure parameters to get fires detected by NASA FIRMS.

data_description.source: Select one or more satellite sources. Using multiple sources gives better temporal coverage. Duplicates are automatically removed.
data_description.daynight: Night detections have fewer false positives from industrial sources (furnaces, gas flaring, city lights). 'All' is recommended for wildfire monitoring.
data_description.radius_km: The search area is always defined in km. Miles only affect how fire distances are displayed in entity states.
data_description.date: Leave empty to use 'days to look back' (real-time mode). Set a date (YYYY-MM-DD) to query a specific historical day instead.
data_description.min_frp: Fire Radiative Power: energy the satellite measures the fire emitting, in Megawatts (MW). <10 MW = small fire or controlled burn. 10-100 MW = active moderate wildfire. 100-1000 MW = large intense fire. >1000 MW = extreme fire (California, Australia scale). Set to 0 to show all detections. Raising to 15-30 MW effectively removes most industrial false positives (furnaces, gas flaring, city lights).

error.api_key_invalid: The API key is invalid. It must be at least 10 characters long.
error.source_required: You must select at least one data source.

abort.already_configured: This integration is already configured for this location.
abort.max_instances_reached: Maximum number of instances reached (10). Remove an existing instance before adding a new one.
